text_blocks.snippet: The cost of dealing with family estrangement in Vancouver, BC, varies significantly based on whether the approach is therapeutic or legal, ranging from a few hundred dollars for counseling to tens of thousands for court battles over child custody or estate disputes. Reconnect Families +1

list.snippet: Individual Counseling: Roughly $150–$175 for a 50–60 minute session.
Family/Couples Counselling: Approximately $190–$200 for a 60-minute session.
Specialized Programs: Intensive programs (e.g., for parental alienation/reunification) can be highly expensive, depending on the length of the intervention.
Mediation: Often used to manage disputes, with fees varying based on the complexity and duration. Reconnect Families +3
Lawyer Hourly Rates: Typically range from $300 to $400 per hour, often requiring a retainer fee starting around $5,000.
Drafting/Reviewing Agreements: A lawyer-drafted separation agreement generally costs between $1,500 and $5,000+, depending on complexity.
Parental Alienation Cases: High-conflict cases requiring court intervention can cost tens of thousands of dollars.
Views of the Child Reports: Used in high-conflict situations, these cost approximately $2,500.

Estate Disputes: If estrangement leads to disinheritance litigation, costs can be significant, although the court may consider the "moral duty" of a parent regardless of the estrangement. disinherited.com +6
Support Obligations: In some cases, despite a broken relationship with a child, courts may still require continued child support payments.
Asset Division: Legal disputes over property division can be expensive, and failure to have a proper, legally reviewed agreement can lead to further litigation costs. ULegal Family Law Group +2

text_blocks.snippet: Note: Some counsellors offer receipts that can be submitted for insurance coverage, while legal fees may be deductible in specific circumstances. Lianne Lab
